September 14, 1990

counsel, Legal Departnent
P.O. Box 8O7
sacramento, california 95804
Attention Blanca Breeze
Proper Financial Disclosure Methods
Dear Ms. Breeze:
with reference to our telephone conversation of this
date, you will recall that I expressed concern over the
question of proper financial disclosure methods for directors
and top staff of The Ii{etropol itan Water District of Southern
California as a result of the amendnent of covernment Code
section 872oo, in particular the addition of the term "other
public officials 
who manage public investments.'r Presently the
subject officials are designated employees under the District's
Conflict of fnterest and DiscLosure of Personal Finances Code
and as such, file annual disclosure statements using form 730.
If section 87200 is found to be applicabLe to these
individuals, they $rould be deleted frorn the District's 
list of
designated employees and tould file fonn 721 directly rdith the
Commiss ion .
Based upon our discussion, it is my understanding
that the regulations interpreting the scope of section B72OO as
amended will not be finalized until Decenber 1990 at the
earliest and possibly not then. Accordingly, the District
should not at this time initiate 
amendnents to our Code to
delete the directors and top staff from the list of designated
enployees who are required to file fonn 730, pending the
adoption of regulations implementing the arnendnents to section
872OO. It is aLso ny understanding that since the period for
annual filing will cornmence January l-, L991, for calendar year
l-990, we should assune that the subject officials wiLl file for
1990 using form ?30 rather than form 721-, and witl continue to
do so until final regulations relating to the arnendnents to
section 87200 are adopted and can be inplemented.
111 Suns(


The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California
-2-
I would appreciate your
understanding of these matters as
JI,IM: db
(jb:FPPC)
confirrnation that ny
discussed above is correct.
S incerely ,
Fred Vendig
eneral Counsel
Deputy
W. Mountain
